summary:
Agent2 considered various molecular biology techniques but concluded only specific types of RNA-sequencing could be said as "directly" working with RNA; however, these still often require initial sample preparation including handling isolated RNAs before analysis continues beyond simple detection stages typical elsewhere such as Western blots focusing more broadly post-transcriptional events linking back ultimately through protein targets instead of remaining strictly within an ongoing RNA context throughout all process phases. Thus, among given choices, [Western Blot] was deemed most fitting despite lacking purest form required by original query constraints regarding unbroken chain dependency upon RNA alone from start till finish during primary operational phase itself. Key point here being distinction between indirect vs direct engagement modes towards core functional objectives pursued using each method type involved compared against idealized scenario posited initially about fully intrinsic focus resting exclusively over entire procedure duration just on manipulating/analyzing native state nucleic acid molecules themselves continually end-to-end. ] Summary:

The agent examined several methods and found that although specialized RNA-seq can work primarily with RNA, they generally need preliminary steps involving RNA manipulations first. Among the listed options, a Western blot better fits because its primary goal involves detecting proteins connected to RNA indirectly, thus avoiding full dependence on RNA at every step of the assay’s operation cycle. The critical difference lies in how closely aligned each test stays with constant interaction specifically centered around undisturbed RNA molecule management versus broader processes engaging downstream products derived therefrom. ]]
